heading: Definitions
label: 21901 Definitions
notes: References in TextThe PROTECT Act, referred to in par. (1), is , , , also known as the Prosecutorial Remedies and Other Tools to end the Exploitation of Children Today Act of 2003. Subtitle A of title III of the Act is classified generally to chapter 205 (§ 20501 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 2003 Amendment note set out under , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and Tables.Pub. children.heading: AMBER Alert communications network
children.label: 1 AMBER Alert communications network
children.text: The term “AMBER Alert communications network” means the AMBER Alert communications network established under subtitle A of title III of the PROTECT Act ( et seq.).34 U.S.C. 20501

children.heading: Ashanti Alert
children.label: 2 Ashanti Alert
children.text: The term “Ashanti Alert” means an alert issued through the Ashanti Alert communications network, related to a missing adult.

children.heading: Ashanti Alert communications network
children.label: 3 Ashanti Alert communications network
children.text: The term “Ashanti Alert communications network” means the national communications network established by the Attorney General under .section 21902(a) of this title

children.heading: Ashanti Alert Coordinator of the Department of Justice; Coordinator
children.label: 4 Ashanti Alert Coordinator of the Department of Justice; Coordinator
children.text: The term “Ashanti Alert Coordinator of the Department of Justice” or “Coordinator” means the employee designated by the Attorney General to act as the national coordinator of the Ashanti Alert communications network under .section 21903(a) of this title

children.heading: Ashanti Alert plan
children.label: 5 Ashanti Alert plan
children.text: The term “Ashanti Alert plan” means a local element of the Ashanti Alert communications network.

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: 6 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” means a federally recognized Indian Tribe or a Native village, Regional Corporation, or Village Corporation (as those terms are defined in ).section 1602 of title 43

children.label: A
children.text: is older than the age for which an alert may be issued through the AMBER Alert communications network in the State or territory of an Indian Tribe in which the individual is identified as a missing individual;

children.label: B
children.text: is identified by a law enforcement agency as a missing individual; and

children.label: C
children.text: meets the requirements to be designated as a missing adult, as determined by the State in which, or the Indian Tribe in the territory of which, the individual is identified as a missing individual.

children.heading: State
children.label: 8 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.
